# ScrewFast - Complete Documentation

## Project Overview

ScrewFast is an **open-source template** designed for quick and efficient web project setup, blending **minimalism with functionality**. Whether you're showcasing a portfolio, launching a company landing page, or running a blog, ScrewFast provides everything you need. By combining the power of the [Astro](https://astro.build/), [Tailwind CSS](https://tailwindcss.com/), and [Preline UI](https://preline.co/), this template offers a functional and aesthetically pleasing solution for your web presence.

**[View Live Demo](https://screwfast.uk)**

### Key Features
- **Astro-Powered:** Utilize a modern static-site generation framework.
- **Tailwind CSS:** Enjoy rapid UI development with a utility-first CSS framework.
- **Preline UI:** Implement prebuilt UI components for added elegance.
- **GSAP Integration:** Impress with professional and polished animations.
- **Markdown Content Collections:** Organize and manage your content seamlessly.
- **Starlight Documentation:** A sleek, user-friendly, full-featured documentation theme.
- **Internationalization (i18n):** Integrates Astro’s internationalization features.
- **SEO and Responsiveness:** Ensure your site is discoverable and accessible on any device.

## Installation & Setup

### Prerequisites
- Node.js (v18 or higher recommended)
- npm or yarn or pnpm

### Getting Started

#### Use This Template
To get started, click the `Use this template` button (the big green one at the top right) to create your own repo from this template in your GitHub account.

#### Clone the Repository
Once your repository is created, you can clone it to your local machine using the following commands:

```bash
git clone https://github.com/[YOUR_USERNAME]/[YOUR_REPO_NAME].git
cd [YOUR_REPO_NAME]
```

### Installation

Start by installing the project dependencies. Open your terminal, navigate to the project's root directory, and execute:

```bash
npm install
```

This command will install all the necessary dependencies defined in the `package.json` file.

### Development Commands

With dependencies installed, you can utilize the following npm scripts to manage your project's development lifecycle:

* `npm run dev`: Starts a local development server with hot reloading enabled.
* `npm run preview`: Serves your build output locally for preview before deployment.
* `npm run build`: Bundles your site into static files for production.

For detailed help with Astro CLI commands, visit [Astro's documentation](https://docs.astro.build/en/reference/cli-reference/).

## Architecture & Design

### Project Structure
ScrewFast follows a modular structure optimized for Astro development:

```
src/
├── assets/               
│   ├── scripts/          # JS scripts (including Lenis smooth scroll)
│   └── styles/           # CSS stylesheets
├── components/           # Reusable components organized by type and function
│   ├── sections/         # Page sections (hero, features, testimonials, etc.)
│   ├── ui/               # Atomic UI components (buttons, forms, icons, etc.)
│   ├── navbar&footer/    # Navigation and footer components
│   └── starlight/        # Starlight documentation theme components
├── content/              # Markdown content collections
│   ├── blog/             # Blog posts (multi-language: en/fr)
│   ├── docs/             # Documentation (for Starlight)
│   ├── insights/         # Insight/articles
│   └── products/         # Product listings
├── data_files/           # JSON data and TypeScript constants
├── images/               # Static image assets
├── layouts/              # Layout components (MainLayout.astro)
├── pages/                # Individual pages routed by file structure
│   ├── blog/             # Blog listing and individual posts
│   ├── fr/               # French localized content
│   ├── insights/         # Insights listing and individual posts
│   ├── products/         # Products listing and individual product pages
│   └── ...               # Other pages (contact, about, services, etc.)
├── utils/                # Utility functions and TypeScript helpers
└── content.config.ts     # Content collections configuration
```

### Design Patterns
- **Component-Based Architecture:** UI broken down into reusable Astro components
- **Content Collections:** Markdown files managed via Astro's content collections for type-safe querying
- **Slot-Based Layouts:** MainLayout.astro uses slots for flexible page composition
- **Internationalization:** Built-in Astro i18n routing with locale prefixes
- **Utility-First Styling:** Tailwind CSS for rapid UI development
- **Progressive Enhancement:** Core functionality works without JS, enhanced with GSAP/Lenis

## API Reference

As a static site generator, ScrewFast doesn't have a traditional API. Instead, it provides:

### Astro Component Interface
All `.astro` files export a default component that can be imported and used in other Astro files.

### Content Collections API
Access content via `getCollection()` function:
```astro
import { getCollection } from 'astro:content';

const blogPosts = await getCollection('blog');
```

### Data Files
Type-safe access to JSON data and constants:
```typescript
import { SITE, SEO, OG } from '@/data_files/constants';
```

### Configuration
Access site configuration via `import.meta.env`:
```typescript
import.meta.env.SITE  // Site URL from astro.config.mjs
```

## Source Code Analysis

### Key Files

#### `src/pages/index.astro`
The main landing page that assembles various sections:
- HeroSection
- FeaturesGeneral
- ClientsSection
- OfferSection
- TestimonialsSection
- FAQ
- CTA sections

#### `src/layouts/MainLayout.astro`
The base layout used by all pages:
- Includes HTML structure, head metadata
- Imports and executes Lenis smooth scroll script
- Provides slot for page content
- Includes Meta component for SEO

#### `src/components/Meta.astro`
Handles SEO metadata:
- Processes structured data, Open Graph tags
- Accepts meta prop for page-specific overrides
- Generates JSON-LD structured data

#### `src/data_files/constants.ts`
Centralized site configuration:
- SITE object: title, tagline, description, URL, author
- SEO object: structured data configuration
- OG object: Open Graph social media tags
- partnersData: placeholder for partner logos

#### `src/content.config.ts`
Defines content collections for:
- blog: blog posts with schema
- insights: insight/articles
- products: product listings
- docs: documentation (for Starlight)

### Component Hierarchy
Pages → Layout → Sections → UI Components → Atoms
Example flow:
index.astro → MainLayout.astro → HeroSection (sections/landing) → PrimaryCTA (ui/buttons) → Button atom

## Configuration

### Astro Configuration (`astro.config.mjs`)
Key settings:
- Site URL: `https://krafferoasters.com`
- Internationalization: English (default) and French
- Integrations:
  - @astrojs/sitemap: Generates XML sitemap with i18n support
  - astro-compressor: Brotli compression (gzip disabled)
  - @astrojs/mdx: MDX support for content
- Experimental: clientPrerender for hybrid rendering
- Vite: Tailwind CSS plugin

### Environment Variables
No required environment variables for basic operation. The template uses:
- `import.meta.env.SITE` from astro.config.mjs
- Optional: API keys for third-party services (to be added in .env)

### Styling Configuration
- Tailwind CSS via `@tailwindcss/vite` plugin
- Prettier plugin for Tailwind class sorting (`.prettierrc`)
- Custom CSS in `src/styles/`:
  - global.css: Base styles and Tailwind imports
  - lenis.css: Styles for Lenis smooth scroll
  - starlight.css/starlight_main.css: Starlight theme overrides

### Content Configuration
Defined in `src/content.config.ts`:
```typescript
export default defineContentConfig({
  collections: {
    blog: { /* schema for blog posts */ },
    insights: { /* schema for insights */ },
    products: { /* schema for products */ },
    docs: { /* schema for Starlight docs */ }
  }
});
```

## Examples & Usage

### Creating a New Page
1. Create a new `.astro` file in `src/pages/` (e.g., `src/pages/test.astro`)
2. Use the MainLayout layout:
```astro
---
import MainLayout from '@/layouts/MainLayout.astro';
---
<MainLayout>
  <h1>Test Page</h1>
  <p>This is a test page.</p>
</MainLayout>
```
3. The page will be accessible at `/test`

### Adding Blog Content
1. Create a markdown file in `src/content/blog/en/`
2. Frontmatter example:
```markdown
---
title: "My Blog Post"
pubDate: 2023-01-01
description: "A brief description"
author: "Author Name"
image:
    url: "/images/blog/post1.jpg"
    alt: "Post image"
tags: ["astro", "webdev"]
---
```
3. Content in Markdown
4. Accessible at `/blog/my-blog-post`

### Customizing Navigation
1. Edit `src/utils/navigation.ts`
2. Modify `navBarLinks` array:
```typescript
export const navBarLinks: NavLink[] = [
  { name: "Home", url: "/" },
  { name: "Products", url: "/products" },
  // ... etc
];
```
3. Changes reflect site-wide via components that import this file

### Starlight Documentation
1. Documentation content lives in `src/content/docs/`
2. Configure via Starlight properties in `astro.config.mjs` (if needed)
3. Accessible at `/docs/` route

### Internationalization
1. Create localized content in language-specific folders:
   - `src/content/blog/en/` for English
   - `src/content/blog/fr/` for French
2. Use `Locale` component for language switching
3. Routes automatically prefixed (except default locale)

## Testing

### Current State
The template does not include automated tests by default. However, it's structured to facilitate testing:

### Recommended Testing Approach
1. **Unit Tests:** Test utility functions in `src/utils/` using Vitest or Jest
2. **Component Tests:** Test Astro components with `@astrojs/check` or manual inspection
3. **End-to-End Tests:** Use Playwright or Cypress for user flow testing
4. **Visual Regression:** Use Chromatic or Percy for UI consistency

### Test Scripts (to be added)
```json
{
  "scripts": {
    "test": "vitest",
    "test:ui": "vitest --ui",
    "e2e": "playwright test"
  }
}
```

### Type Checking
The template includes:
- TypeScript support via `tsconfig.json`
- Astro type checking: `astro check`
- Prettier plugin for Tailwind ensures consistent formatting

## Dependencies

### Core Dependencies
| Package | Purpose | Version |
|---------|---------|---------|
| astro | Static site generator | ^5.6.1 |
| @astrojs/sitemap | XML sitemap generation | ^3.3.0 |
| @astrojs/mdx | MDX support | ^4.2.3 |
| @astrojs/starlight | Documentation theme | ^0.32.5 |
| @tailwindcss/vite | Tailwind CSS integration | ^4.1.3 |
| tailwindcss | Utility-first CSS framework | ^4.1.3 |
| preline | Prebuilt UI components | ^3.0.1 |
| gsap | Animation library | ^3.12.7 |
| lenis | Smooth scrolling | ^1.2.3 |
| astro-compressor | HTML compression (Brotli) | ^1.0.0 |
| html-minifier-terser | HTML minification | ^7.2.0 |
| sharp | Image optimization | ^0.34.0 |
| clipboard | Clipboard API polyfill | ^2.0.11 |
| globby | File pattern matching | ^14.1.0 |

### Dev Dependencies
| Package | Purpose | Version |
|---------|---------|---------|
| prettier | Code formatting | ^3.5.3 |
| prettier-plugin-astro | Astro formatting for Prettier | ^0.14.1 |
| prettier-plugin-tailwindcss | Tailwind CSS class sorting | ^0.6.11 |
| astro-vtbot | View transitions toolkit | ^2.1.2 |
| @tailwindcss/forms | Tailwind forms plugin | ^0.5.10 |
| typescript | TypeScript language | ^5.8.3 |

## Development Guide

### Code Style
- Follow Prettier configuration (`.prettierrc`)
- Tailwind CSS class ordering via plugin
- TypeScript strict mode enabled in `tsconfig.json`
- Component props should be typed when possible

### Git Workflow
1. Create feature branch from `main`
2. Make changes with clear, descriptive commits
3. Submit pull request with description of changes
4. Ensure CI/CD passes (if configured)

### Adding New Features
1. **UI Components:** Add to appropriate directory in `src/components/ui/`
2. **Page Sections:** Add to `src/components/sections/` with relevant subdirectory
3. **Content Types:** Extend `src/content.config.ts` with new collection
4. **Data:** Add JSON to `src/data_files/` or constants to `constants.ts`
5. **Styles:** Add to `src/styles/` or use Tailwind utility classes directly

### Performance Optimization
- Image optimization via Astro's built-in Image component
- HTML compression via astro-compressor (Brotli)
- Lazy loading: Use `loading="lazy"` on images
- Font optimization: Consider self-hosting fonts
- Bundle analysis: Use `astro build --profile`

### Deployment
The template is optimized for:
- **Vercel:** Zero-config deployment with `vercel.json`
- **Netlify:** Configured via `netlify.toml`
- **Other platforms:** Build output in `dist/` directory after `npm run build`

### Contributing
See the full `CONTRIBUTING` section in README_ORIGINAL_THEME.md:
1. Report issues via GitHub Issues
2. Submit pull requests with clear descriptions
3. Provide feedback on existing features
4. Follow the code style guidelines
